justification.personality_voice: Across all six books, the films preserve Ron's surface personality reliably: the humor, the loyalty, the bluntness, the reactive sarcasm. PoA and CoS score highest here (18-20) because the core voice is intact and the losses are secondary. The floor is set by OotP (14), where the removal of the prefect arc, Quidditch arc, and Percy letter strips the sustained texture of his personality - the specific pattern of humor-as-deflection, avoidance, and unexpected moral clarity. GoF (17) and HBP (18) sit in the middle: the surface is right but the sustained emotional ugliness (prolonged jealousy, cruelty to Hermione) is compressed into brief beats. The films consistently preserve Ron's two most visible traits (funny, loyal) while removing the traits that complicate them (petty, resentful, capable of sustained unkindness). The synthesis score of 17 reflects a portrayal that is recognizable across all films but systematically softened - the rough edges that make book-Ron a three-dimensional character are present in fragments but never sustained.
justification.narrative_role_agency: The most consistent failure across all six films is the reduction of Ron's active agency. The pattern is clear: Ron's investigative contributions are transferred to Hermione or simply cut (Mudblood explanation in CoS, Buckbeak research in PoA, cave meeting in GoF, ghoul plan in DH); his most courageous individual moments are transferred to Hermione (Shrieking Shack line in PoA); his independent narrative threads are removed entirely (Quidditch arc in OotP, Norbert subplot in PS, Crouch investigation in GoF). The best book for Ron's agency is DH (14), where the locket destruction, Chamber plan, and Deluminator are preserved. The worst is OotP (11), where three major independent threads are entirely absent. The synthesis score of 13 reflects a character who remains present and participates in group scenes but whose individual acts of initiative, investigation, and courage are systematically reduced or reassigned across the entire series.
justification.motivations_internal_conflict: The films preserve Ron's surface motivations (loyalty to Harry, family protectiveness) but consistently remove the internal conflicts that give those motivations texture. The class anxiety underpinning his jealousy in GoF (leprechaun gold, 'I hate being poor') is absent. The prefect insecurity in OotP is absent. The Lavender/Hermione emotional cowardice in HBP is compressed. The Horcrux-amplified insecurity in DH is the best-rendered internal conflict across the series. The pattern is that the films show Ron's motivations in their simplest form - he supports Harry because he is Harry's friend - without the specific, book-established insecurities that make those motivations psychologically real. Score of 10 reflects motivations that are correct in direction but consistently flattened in depth.
justification.character_arc: OotP is the most damaging book for Ron's arc: the three arc components (prefect, Quiddcher, Percy) are entirely absent, leaving him flat by omission. GoF's arc is present but so compressed that the estrangement reads as a brief misunderstanding rather than a weeks-long rupture. HBP's arc is similarly compressed - the sustained ugliness that makes the reconciliation earned is shortened. The best-rendered arc across the series is DH (12), where the departure-return-redemption structure is preserved, though the middle growth phase is missing. PS and CoS arcs are modest by design and mostly preserved. The synthesis score of 10 reflects a series-wide pattern of arc compression: the films show the beginning and end of Ron's arc movements but skip the sustained middle sections that give them emotional weight.
justification.key_relationships: The Harry-Ron friendship is the most consistently preserved relationship across all six films - it is the one dimension where the films rarely fail Ron significantly. The Ron-Hermione relationship is present in all films but consistently loses its specific emotional texture: the sustained feud in PoA, the cruelty during the Lavender period in HBP, the complex jealousy in GoF. The family relationships (Percy rupture in OotP, class anxiety in GoF, family fear in DH) are present but thin. The Sirius relationship in GoF is entirely absent. The synthesis score of 7 reflects relationships that are functionally intact but emotionally simplified - the films show that Ron cares about these people without fully showing why or how those relationships reveal his character.
justification.complexity_nuance_lost_material: The most consistent series-wide loss is the material that makes Ron uncomfortable to watch: his sustained pettiness in GoF, his cruelty to Hermione in HBP, his avoidance and self-deception in OotP. The films systematically remove the scenes where Ron is genuinely unsympathetic for extended periods, replacing them with brief comic beats that are easier to forgive. Secondary losses include his independent moral voice (Mudblood explanation in CoS), his proactive planning (ghoul plan in DH, Buckbeak research in PoA), and his class-anxiety interiority (GoF). The result across the series is a Ron who is funnier and more immediately likable than his book counterpart but who lacks the psychological depth and uncomfortable edges that make book-Ron a genuinely complex character. Score of 8 reflects noticeable, cumulative simplification.

lost_or_transferred_material: PoA: 'If you want to kill Harry, you'll have to kill us too' transferred to Hermione
CoS: Mudblood explanation transferred to Hermione
OotP: Prefect badge arc entirely absent
OotP: Quidditch Keeper arc ('Weasley is our King') entirely absent
OotP: Percy letter scene entirely absent
GoF: Leprechaun gold / 'I hate being poor' scene absent
GoF: Cave meeting with Sirius absent
GoF: Confrontation with Fred and George about blackmail absent
DH: Extended leadership during Harry's Hallows obsession absent
PS: Norbert subplot (dragon bite, hospital wing, Malfoy leverage) absent
PS: Post-points-loss loyalty sequence ('only Ron stood by him') absent
PoA: Buckbeak appeal research initiative absent
HBP: Sustained cruelty to Hermione during Lavender period compressed to brief beats
score_caps_applied: PoA: Agency transfer cap applied (Shrieking Shack courage line transferred to Hermione)
comparative.key_observations: Across six films, Ron Weasley is the character most consistently diminished by adaptation compression. The films preserve his two most visible traits - humor and loyalty - with reasonable fidelity, but systematically remove everything that complicates them. The pattern is not random: the films cut the scenes where Ron is petty for weeks (GoF estrangement), cruel to people he loves (HBP Lavender period), independently ambitious (OotP Quidditch arc), morally vocal (CoS Mudblood explanation), or proactively clever (DH ghoul plan, PoA Buckbeak research). What remains is a Ron who is always supportive, always funny, and never genuinely unsympathetic - a simplified version that is easier to like but harder to believe. The single most damaging individual adaptation decision across the series is the transfer of his Shrieking Shack courage line to Hermione in PoA, which removes his defining act of bravery in that film. The most damaging book overall is OotP, where three major character-defining arcs are entirely absent. The synthesis score of 65 reflects a portrayal that is recognizable and not unfaithful in its broad strokes, but which has been systematically softened, compressed, and simplified in ways that collectively reduce Ron from a complex, contradictory, fully human character to a lovable sidekick function.
